rationale.R1.2: Any meaningful opinion on a research paper requires verifying that premises (lexical ambiguity degrades zero-shot LLM translation), intermediates (two-stage prompting surfaces intent), and conclusions (COMET/human eval gains are meaningful) are mutually consistent; this is a natural load-bearing read of 'what do you think'.
rationale.R1.3: The paper makes strong causal claims—disambiguation context yields gains—backed by COMET-22 deltas, an ablation table, and a human preference rate; whether those numbers actually support those claims is a natural load-bearing read of 'what do you think'.
rationale.R1.4: The paper explicitly asserts two firsts ('first translation framework…', 'first to demonstrate…'); evaluating whether those novelty claims survive comparison to nearest prior work (e.g., interactive MT, clarification-before-decoding methods) is a natural load-bearing read of 'what do you think'.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you primarily asking whether ClariNMT's novelty claims hold up against prior work (R1.4 load-bearing), or whether the reported empirical results adequately support the causal conclusions the authors draw (R1.3 load-bearing)?
